perturbation.original_text: Recipient may disclose the Product only to its employees who have a need to know in order to accomplish the purposes identified in Section l(a), and such employees' use of the Product shall take place solely at Recipient's site. Recipient will have executed appropriate written agreements with its employees sufficient to enable it to comply with the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient may disclose the Product only to its employees to accomplish the purposes identified in Section l(a), and such employees' use of the Product shall take place solely at Recipient's site. Recipient will ensure its employees comply with the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'who have a need to know' and 'Recipient will have executed appropriate written agreements with its employees sufficient to enable it to comply with the terms of this Agreement' from section 1(c), it weakens the confidentiality requirements. Section 6 relies on this clause for enforcement of confidentiality, making the contract internally inconsistent.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Product and related information is proprietary and confidential information to MS and its suppliers. Recipient agrees not to disclose or provide the Product, documentation, or any related information (including the Product features or the results of use or testing) to any third party or use the Product for any purpose other than as provided in this Agreement. However, Recipient may disclose confidential information in accordance with judicial or other governmental order, provided Recipient shall give MS reasonable written notice prior to such disclosure and shall comply with any applicable protective order or equivalent. Further, Recipient shall not be obligated to maintain the confidentiality of information which Recipient can prove (1) is already known to Recipient without an obligation to maintain the same as confidential; (2) becomes publicly known through no wrongful act of Recipient; (3) is rightfully received from a third party without breach of an obligation of confidentiality owed to MS; or (4) is independently developed by Recipient. This provision shall survive the termination or expiration of this Agreement with respect to any information that has not been made public by MS as of the commercial release of the Product.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly limits disclosure of the Product to employees 'who have a need to know' and requires written agreements to ensure compliance. The changed text removes these safeguards, weakening confidentiality. The contradicted text emphasizes the confidential nature of the Product and prohibits disclosure. By removing the 'need to know' restriction and the requirement for written agreements, the modified text creates a contradiction because it broadens the scope of permissible disclosure, potentially conflicting with the explicit confidentiality obligations outlined in the contradicted section. This makes it harder to enforce confidentiality compared to the initial contract.

perturbation.original_text: MS further grants to recipient the following non-exclusive, nontransferable, royalty-free rights with respect to the sample and redistributable code listed in the readme.txt file. (a) Subject to Section 2(b) below, to reproduce and distribute test version of Recipient's Application for use on The Microsoft Network ("MSN") created using the Product to MSN users provided you identify such Application as "BETA" and that you comply with Section 2(c), below. (b) To modify the sample code provided with the Product and to reproduce and distribute such modifications in object code form for use on The Microsoft Network to MSN users provided you identify such Application as "BETA" and that you comply with Section 2(c), below.
perturbation.changed_text: MS further grants to recipient the rights with respect to the sample and redistributable code. (a) Subject to Section 2(b) below, to reproduce and distribute test version of Recipient's Application for use on The Microsoft Network ("MSN") created using the Product to MSN users. (b) To modify the sample code provided with the Product and to reproduce and distribute such modifications in object code form for use on The Microsoft Network to MSN users.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'non-exclusive, nontransferable, royalty-free' and 'provided you identify such Application as "BETA" and that you comply with Section 2(c), below.' in sections 2(a) and 2(b). It contradicts the later clauses of the sections.
perturbation.contradicted_text: if you redistribute your Application as allowed under this Agreement, you must: (1) distribute the Product only in conjunction with and as part of your Application which is designed, developed and tested to operate on MSN; (2) not make any statements to the effect or which imply that your Application is "certified" by MS or that its performance is guaranteed by MS; (3) not use MS' name, logo, or trademarks to market your Application; and (4) agree to indemnify, hold harmless, and defend MS and its suppliers from and against any claims or lawsuits, including attorney's fees, that arise or result from your distribution of the Application.
perturbation.justification: The original text grants 'non-exclusive, nontransferable, royalty-free rights' and includes the condition to identify the application as 'BETA' and comply with section 2(c). The changed text removes these limitations. The contradicted text imposes obligations regarding redistribution of the application, implying limitations similar to those removed (e.g., distributing only with MSN compatibility, disclaimers about MS certification, trademark restrictions, and indemnification). Removing the 'non-exclusive, nontransferable, royalty-free' designation and the 'BETA' and compliance clause from the granting clause directly contradicts the spirit and intent of the obligations imposed in the contradicted section, making the scope of permitted redistribution unclear and potentially conflicting.

perturbation.original_text: The Product shall not be used, modified, reproduced except as provided above and you agree to destroy or erase the copy of the Product upon MS' release of the Product in final form.
perturbation.changed_text: The Product shall not be used, modified, reproduced except as provided above and you agree to return the copy of the Product upon MS' release of the Product in final form.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'destroy or erase' to 'return', the agreement creates contradiction with Section 3.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The term of this Agreement shall commence on the Effective Date and shall continue until terminated by MS in writing at any time, with or without cause. This Agreement will terminate without notice upon the commercial release of the Product. Upon the termination of this Agreement, Recipient shall promptly return to MS. or certify destruction of, all full or partial copies of the Product and related materials provided by MS. Section 6 shall survive termination or expiration of this Agreement with respect to any information that has not been made public by MS as of the commercial release of the Product.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the recipient must 'destroy or erase' the copy of the product. The changed text requires the recipient to 'return' the copy of the product. Section 3 states that upon termination, the recipient shall 'return to MS. or certify destruction of, all full or partial copies of the Product.' The change from 'destroy or erase' to 'return' in the modified text creates a contradiction because the original text offers two options, while the changed text only offers one. This is contradictory to Section 3, because now only 'return' is specified, while Section 3 allows for the certification of destruction.

perturbation.original_text: MS is not obligated to provide maintenance or updates to Recipient for the Product. However, any maintenance or updates provided by MS shall be covered by this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: MS is not obligated to provide maintenance or updates to Recipient for the Product.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'However, any maintenance or updates provided by MS shall be covered by this Agreement.', removes a key clause and will later create a contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Product is deemed accepted by Recipient. The Product constitutes pre-release code and may be changed substantially before commercial release. The PRODUCT IS PROVIDED "AS IS" WITHOUT WARRANTY OF ANY KIND. TO THE MAXIMUM EXTENT PERMITTED BY APPLICABLE LAW, MICROSOFT FURTHER DISCLAIMS ALL WARRANTIES, INCLUDING WITHOUT LIMITATION ANY IMPLIED WARRANTIES OF MERCHANTABILITY, FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE, AND NONINFRINGEMENT. THE ENTIRE RISK ARISING OUT OF THE USE OR PERFORMANCE OF THE PRODUCT AND DOCUMENTATION REMAINS WITH RECIPIENT. TO THE MAXIMUM EXTENT PERMITTED BY APPLICABLE LAW, IN NO EVENT SHALL MICROSOFT OR ITS SUPPLIERS BE LIABLE FOR ANY CONSEQUENTIAL, INCIDENTAL, DIRECT, INDIRECT, SPECIAL, PUNITIVE, OR OTHER DAMAGES WHATSOEVER (INCLUDING, WITHOUT LIMITATION, DAMAGES FOR LOSS OF BUSINESS PROFITS, BUSINESS INTERRUPTION, LOSS OF BUSINESS INFORMATION, OR OTHER PECUNIARY LOSS) ARISING OUT OF THIS AGREEMENT OR THE USE OF OR INABILITY TO USE THE PRODUCT, EVEN IF MICROSOFT HAS BEEN ADVISED OF THE POSSIBILITY OF SUCH DAMAGES. BECAUSE SOME STATES/JURISDICTIONS DO NOT ALLOW THE EXCLUSION OR LIMITATION OF LIABILITY FOR CONSEQUENTIAL OR INCIDENTAL DAMAGES, THE ABOVE LIMITATION MAY NOT APPLY TO RECIPIENT.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that while MS is not obligated to provide updates, if they do, the agreement covers them. The changed text removes the clause about updates being covered by the agreement. The contradicted text provides a disclaimer of warranty and liability. By removing the clause about maintenance/updates being covered, it implies that MS provides NO coverage if there are any maintenance or updates even though the original agreement and contradicted text mentions "AS IS" condition. This creates a strong contradiction because without the 'however' clause, any updates are now potentially outside the scope of the agreement, conflicting with the liability disclaimer and creating ambiguity and a strong contradiction about support.
